New NVIDIA Neatron 3 is insane. Nvidia just dropped something massive and almost nobody is talking about it. A free open AI model that runs multi- aent systems five times faster, handles a million tokens of context without breaking a sweat, and the biggest AI companies in the world are already using it. This changes how we build AI agents forever. Let me break it all down right now. Now, let's talk about why this model is a really big deal. Because if you're building AI agents or you want to, this is the most important release of the year so far. And I'm going to show you exactly why step by step. So Nvidia just released a model called Neatron 3 Super. It is a 120 billion parameter model, but here is what makes it special. It only uses 12 billion [music] active parameters at any one time. What does that mean? It means the model is huge in terms of what it knows, but it runs lean, fast, and efficient. Think of it like a Formula 1 car that gets the fuel economy of a Toyota Camry. You get the power without the cost. And for Aentic AI, that matters more than almost anything else. Here is why. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. When you build AI agents, you hit two massive walls really fast. Wall number one is called context explosion. Normal AI chat, a model sends a message and you get a reply. Simple. But multi- aent systems are completely different. Every single step, the agent has to resend its full history, all the tool outputs, all the intermediate thinking, all the results from every subtask it ran. Nvidia says this creates up to 15 times more tokens than a normal chat interaction. A multi- aent run might cost 15. And over long tasks, something even worse happens. The agent starts to drift. It forgets what the original goal was. It wanders. It makes decisions that don't connect back to what you actually asked for. That is called goal drift and it kills the usefulness of agents fast. Wall number two is the thinking tax. Complex agents have to reason at every step. Should I search the web now? Should I summarize? Should I write the next section? Should I call this tool? If you use a massive model to make every single one of those decisions, it gets very slow and very expensive very fast. Most multi-agent apps right now are not practical at scale because of exactly this problem. You either spend a fortune or the agent is too slow to use in production. Now, if you want the full process, LOPS and over a 100 AI use cases like this one, join the AI profit boardroom. Learn how to automate your workflows with tools like Nvidia Neatron 3 and scale your business with AI automation. The link is in the comments and description. Neatron 3 Super is designed to solve both of these problems at the same time. Let me show you how. First, it has a 1 million token context window. 1 million tokens. That means your agent can hold its full workflow state in memory for a very long time without losing track of anything. No gold drift, no forgetting what the original task was. The agent stays sharp across the entire job. For the AI profit boardroom, imagine running an agent that manages your entire content calendar, handles your email follow-ups, and tracks your community metrics all in a single workflow without ever losing the thread. That is what a million token context window makes possible. Second, the model is built for speed at lower cost. NVIDIA says it delivers five times higher throughput compared to similar models of the same size. Five times more work for roughly the same cost. That is the kind of efficiency that lets you actually deploy agents in production, not just demo them in a notebook. And it is already hitting benchmarks. Artificial Analysis ranked it at the top for efficiency and openness with leading accuracy among models in its weight class. I want to stay with the benchmark numbers for just a second because they are genuinely impressive. And then I want to show you who's already using this in the real world. Nvidia used Nematron 3 Super to power their AIQ research agent and that agent just hit number one on the deep research bench leaderboard and number one on deep research bench tire. These are benchmarks that test whether an AI can do real deep multi-step research across massive document sets while staying logically coherent the whole time. Not just answering a quick question, but actually doing the kind of sustained rigorous research that would take a human analyst hours or days. And this model is doing it faster and more accurately than anything else currently on those leaderboards. Now, here's where I want you to pay attention because the companies already using this tell you everything about where AI agents are going. Perplexity, the AI search engine, is giving their users direct access to Neatron 3 Super for search and is one of 20 orchestrated models inside their computer product. Code Rabbit, Factory AI, and GPile are all integrating it into their software development agents using it alongside their own proprietary models to get higher accuracy at a lower cost. That is a hybrid approach that is going to dominate agent architectures over the next 12 months. Edison Science and Laya Sciences are using it for deep literature search, data science and molecular understanding in life sciences running complex research agents in one of the most demanding fields there is. And on the enterprise side, Amdocs, Palanteer, Cadence, Dassel systems and Seammens are all deploying and customizing the model for telecom cyber security semiconductor design and manufacturing workflows. When Palanteer and Seammens are both running the same open model in production, you know it is something real. Now, let me get practical because I want to show you what this actually looks like when you put it to work. The key insight here is that Neatron 3 Super is built for orchestration. That means it works best not as a single model answering a single question, but as a reasoning layer inside a multi- aent system. Here is a real example of how you could use this for a business like the AI profit boardroom. You set up a research agent powered by Neatron 3 Super and its job is to monitor the AI space daily, pull the most important new developments, cross reference them against what has already been covered in the community, and then draft a priorities briefing for what the boardroom members should know about this week. That agent needs to hold a lot of context. It needs to remember what was covered last week. It needs to reason about what is genuinely new versus what is noise. It needs to stay on task across multiple tool calls. A standard model falls apart here. Either it drifts or it is too slow or it costs too much to run daily. Neatron 3 super with its million token window and five times throughput advantage can actually do this job reliably in production. And this is not just theoretical. The architecture for this kind of workflow already exists. Tools like Langraph, Autogen and Crewai let you plug models into multi- aent frameworks. You can point those frameworks at Neatron 3 Super via Nvidia's NIM microservices or through APAs where the model is already available. Perplexity already showed you it works for search. Code Rabbit showed you it works for code review. The pattern is the same across every vertical. You define the goal, you set up the tools, the agent reasons through the task using Neatron 3 Super as the brain and it does it cheaper and faster than almost any alternative at that accuracy level. Let me also talk about something that matters a lot if you are building with AI for your business. This model is open and that is not a small thing. Open means you can run it yourself, fine-tune it on your own data, and customize it for your specific workflows without sending everything to a third party API. For businesses that handle sensitive data or that want to avoid being locked into one vendor's pricing forever, open models are a massive strategic advantage. Enterprise players like Cadence and Seammens are not deploying this because they needed one more chatbot. They are deploying it because they can own the stack. They can customize and they can run it on their own infrastructure. That is a different category of AI deployment than using a consumer API. And Neatron 3 Super brings that capability to a weight class that is actually practical to self-host.
